paper_map.title: Low-density Fe40Mn19Ni15Al15Si10C1 high entropy steel processed by mechanical alloying and spark plasma sintering: Phase evolution, microstructure and mechanical properties
paper_map.abstract_summary: The present study synthesizes a non-equiatomic Fe40Mn19Ni15Al15Si10C1 low-density high entropy steel via mechanical alloying and spark plasma sintering. The milled powder forms multiphase BCC, τ-brass, and B2 phases, and the sintered bulk retains these with additional Fe5Si3 silicide, achieving low density (~6.49 g/cm³), high microhardness (~7.8 GPa), high compressive strength (~2046 MPa), and ~19% ductility. Enhanced properties result from dual-phase microstructure, nano-silicides, and dominant grain-boundary and dislocation strengthening.
paper_map.research_objective: To design a low-density, dual-phase (BCC + B2) high entropy steel with superior strength-ductility synergy and thermal stability via mechanical alloying and spark plasma sintering, and to correlate phase evolution, microstructure, and strengthening mechanisms.
material_systems: Fe5Si3-type silicide (hP16, a=b=0.667 nm, c=0.468 nm)
expected_information_axis: mechanical properties (hardness, compressive strength, ductility, elastic modulus)
strengthening mechanisms (grain boundary, dislocation, precipitation, solid solution)

key_sections.heading: Introduction
key_sections.purpose: Background on high entropy alloys/steels, motivation for dual-phase BCC+B2 low-density HES via MA and SPS

key_sections.heading: Experimental details
key_sections.purpose: Description of MA and SPS processing parameters, characterization techniques

key_sections.heading: Results
key_sections.purpose: Phase evolution, microstructure, thermal stability, mechanical properties data

key_sections.heading: Discussion (4.1 Alloying behavior and microstructure, 4.2 Thermal stability, 4.3 Mechanical properties and strengthening mechanisms)
key_sections.purpose: Detailed analysis of alloying behavior, phase stability, and strengthening mechanisms (grain boundary, dislocation, precipitation, solid solution); comparison with literature

key_sections.heading: Conclusion
key_sections.purpose: Summary of findings: phase constitution, mechanical properties, and mechanisms
